title: Anacamptis × olida
taxonomy: Anacamptis × olida is a hybrid species resulting from the cross between Anacamptis morio and Orchis olivieri. This hybrid orchid is classified within the family Orchidaceae. The classification of Anacamptis × olida is further detailed by its membership in the genus Anacamptis, which is a group of terrestrial orchids. The species is also classified as a hybrid within the orchid subfamily Orchidoideae. Its exact taxonomic classification is Anacamptis × olida within the plant kingdom.

intro: Anacamptis × olida is a hybrid orchid species, resulting from the cross between Anacamptis morio and Anacamptis papillonacea, characterized by its unique and varied flowering patterns. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Anacamptis × olida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Anacamptis × olida is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are traditionally employed to treat respiratory and skin disorders, as well as to boost the immune system.
